evals.prompt: ok I've been working on adding a dark mode toggle to the settings page. I got the toggle component wired up and it saves to localStorage but the CSS variables aren't switching yet — I need to update the theme provider. also I decided to use CSS custom properties instead of Tailwind's dark: prefix because we need runtime switching. let's checkpoint this before I close the terminal
evals.expected_output: A checkpoint file in logs/ that captures: what was built (toggle component, localStorage persistence), what's not done (CSS variable switching, theme provider update), the design decision (CSS custom properties over Tailwind dark:), and specific next steps about wiring up the theme provider
expectations: A markdown file exists in logs/ with a timestamp-based filename
The file contains YAML frontmatter with date, project, and branch fields
The file has a 'Next steps' section with numbered action items
Next steps contain at least one specific reference (file path, component name, or function name)
The checkpoint records the design decision about CSS custom properties vs Tailwind dark: prefix
The checkpoint captures that CSS variable switching is incomplete and the theme provider still needs updating
No sections are present with empty or placeholder content

evals.prompt: checkpoint — I spent this session trying to figure out why the API response times spiked after the last deploy. Looked at the logs, found that the new middleware is running a DB query on every request instead of caching the auth check. didn't fix it yet but I know exactly where the problem is — it's in src/middleware/auth.ts line 23 where it calls validateSession() without checking the cache first
evals.expected_output: A checkpoint for an investigation-only session. Should use Key findings (not What changed), record the root cause at src/middleware/auth.ts:23, and have fix-oriented next steps
expectations: A markdown file exists in logs/ with a timestamp-based filename
The file contains YAML frontmatter with date, project, and branch fields
The file has a 'Next steps' section with numbered action items
The checkpoint mentions src/middleware/auth.ts and line 23 or validateSession()
The checkpoint does NOT have a 'What changed' section (since no code was modified)
The checkpoint has a 'Key findings' section capturing the root cause discovery
Next steps focus on implementing the cache check fix, not on further investigation

evals.prompt: save session — I was just reviewing the project structure and reading through the docs to understand how the plugin system works. no code changes, just learning. the key thing I figured out is that plugins register via a manifest.json in their root and the loader in core/plugin-loader.ts discovers them by scanning the plugins/ directory at startup
evals.expected_output: A lean checkpoint for an informational session. Should use Key findings for learnings, omit What changed and Decisions, and have actionable next steps
expectations: A markdown file exists in logs/ with a timestamp-based filename
The file contains YAML frontmatter with date, project, and branch fields
The checkpoint mentions manifest.json and core/plugin-loader.ts as key learnings
The checkpoint does NOT have a 'What changed' section
The checkpoint has a 'Key findings' section with what was learned
The checkpoint is noticeably lean — no padding with empty or irrelevant sections
Next steps exist and are oriented toward actionable follow-up
